topic: An employee is asked to train their replacement
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed, magnifying every slight tremor in Sarah’s hands as she smoothed down her already perfectly ironed blouse. Mark, the department head, leaned back in his chair, a relaxed smile playing on his lips. "Sarah, I need you to run through the Q3 reporting process with Alex. He's taking over next week." Sarah felt a sudden, hot flush creep up her neck. She glanced down at the stack of binders on her desk, suddenly intensely aware of the slight smudge of ink on the corner of the top sheet. "Oh. Of course, Mark," she managed, her voice sounding oddly thin. Internally, a frantic checklist of potential errors flashed through her mind. She worried about tripping over her own feet, about stumbling over the jargon she had meticulously memorized.

[story 2]

"So, you'll be mentoring Ben," Mr. Henderson announced, tapping a pen against the laminated training schedule. The air in the conference room seemed to thicken, pressing down on Liam. He shifted in his seat, crossing his arms tightly over his chest, trying to make his posture seem more authoritative than he felt. Every time he looked at Ben, he noticed the unfamiliarity of the task—the responsibility of being the knowledgeable one, the one who had to be flawless. He kept fiddling with the cuff of his shirt, his gaze darting toward the window as if searching for an escape route. "Yes, sir," Liam replied, his tone clipped and overly formal. He focused intently on the notes in front of him, desperate to appear competent, terrified that any misplaced word would expose the gap in his knowledge.

[story 3]

"It's a big step, Sarah," Maria said, her expression warm but serious. "You'll be guiding David through the client onboarding. He needs someone steady." Sarah swallowed hard, the dryness in her throat making the action feel monumental. She smoothed her skirt for the tenth time, a nervous habit she couldn't shake. When Maria mentioned David, Sarah felt a distinct tightening in her stomach, a physical knot that made breathing shallow. She forced herself to meet Maria’s eyes, offering a small, tight nod. "I... I can certainly try," she murmured, wishing the floor would simply swallow her whole. She imagined David watching her, cataloging every hesitation, every moment where her practiced confidence might crack.
